records.ety: [OE. wrethe, AS. wrǣð a twisted band, fr. wrīðan to twist. See Writhe.]

defs: Something twisted, intertwined, or curled; as, a wreath of smoke; a wreath of flowers.
quotes: [He] of his tortuous train Curled many a wanton wreath.

defs: A garland; a chaplet, esp. one given to a victor.
quotes: Conquest doth grant He dear wreath to the Grecian combatant.
Far back in the ages, The plow with wreaths was crowned.

defs: An appendage to the shield, placed above it, and supporting the crest (see Illust. of Crest). It generally represents a twist of two cords of silk, one tinctured like the principal metal, the other like the principal color in the arms.
